title: Proteomic Biomarkers for Bisphenol A-Early Exposure and Women's Thyroid Cancer.
ehr_evidence: None. The study uses mouse models and clinical samples from patients.
summary: The study investigates the effects of early-life Bisphenol A (BPA) exposure on thyroid proteins in mice and identifies ANXA6 and VCP as potential proteomic biomarkers associated with thyroid cancer in women.
key_findings: 17 proteins were altered in a dose-dependent manner in the thyroid tissues of offspring mice.
Positive association between blood BPA levels and mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP in thyroid cancer tissues (but not in normal tissues).
captured_features: mRNA expression of ANXA6 and VCP
study_design: Experimental (mouse model) and clinical biomarker analysis
population: Female ICR mice (offspring) and 28 women thyroid cancer patients
limitations: Reliance on animal models to establish early-life exposure mechanisms
